title: Methods using photothermal nanoparticles in rapid nucleic acid amplification and photothermal nanoparticles
abstract: Methods using photothermal nanoparticles in rapid nucleic acid amplification and photothermal nanoparticles include forming a reaction mixture comprising a nucleic acid template, a polymerase enzyme, and a heating media. The heating media comprises a plurality of photothermal nanoparticles suspended in a solution, fabricated on a microchip, or on a surface of a well in a multi-well plate. The photothermal nanoparticles are nanoparticles having a particular geometric shape and comprise a single component or a multi-component. The plurality of photothermal nanoparticles are further categorized by surface plasmon resonance with a resonance wavelength in a particular range to convert energy absorbed from a light source to sufficiently heat a volume of a composition for the transcription of the new nucleic acid strand. The particular geometric shape of the photothermal nanoparticles is selected from the group consisting of: polyhedral, spheroid, torus, and hollow shapes.
claims:
Claims What is claimed, without limitation: 1. A method for forming a reaction mixture comprising a nucleic acid template, a polymerase enzyme, and a heating media comprising a plurality of photothermal nanoparticles suspended in a solution, fabricated on a microchip, or on a surface of a well in a multi-well plate, wherein the photothermal nanoparticles are nanoparticles having a particular geometric shape and comprise a single component or a multi-component, wherein the plurality of photothermal nanoparticles are further categorized by surface plasmon resonance with a resonance wavelength in a particular range to convert energy absorbed from a light source to sufficiently heat a volume of a composition for transcription of a new nucleic acid strand, and wherein the particular geometric shape is selected from the group consisting of: polyhedral, spheroid, toms, and hollow shapes. 2. A method comprising either forming a suspension of photothermal nanoparticles in solution or immobilizing photothermal nanoparticles on a substrate surface. 3. A method for rapid amplification of a nucleic acid molecule with a polymerase chain reaction comprising: forming a reaction mixture including a nucleic acid template, a polymerase enzyme, and a heating media including photothermal nanoparticles suspended in a solution, fabricated on a microchip, or on a surface of a well in a multi-well plate, wherein the photothermal nanoparticles have a particular geometric shape and comprise a single component or a multi- component, and the photothermal nanoparticles are further categorized by surface plasmon resonance with a resonance wavelength in a particular range to convert energy absorbed from a light source to sufficiently heat a volume of a composition for transcription of a new nucleic acid strand, and wherein the particular geometric shape is selected from the group consisting of: polyhedral, spheroid, toms, and hollow shapes; and hollow shapes. irradiating the reaction mixture at a wavelength ranging from 300 nm to 1500 nm, depending on the particular geometric shape of the photothermal nanoparticles, sufficient for heating the reaction mixture at a rate up to 75 degrees Celsius per second to complete amplification thermal cycles in 15 minutes or less time by adjusting a concentration of photothermal nanoparticles. 4. The method of claim 3, comprising forming a reaction mixture including a nucleic acid template, a polymerase enzyme, and heating media including photothermal nanoparticles fabricated on a microchip or on the surface of a plate or well of a multi-well plate, wherein the photothermal nanoparticles have a particular geometry. 5. The method of claim 3, comprising irradiating the photothermal nanoparticles to heat up the reaction mixture for rapid amplification of a nucleic acid molecule. 6. The method of claim 5, comprising irradiating the whole reaction mixture, half the reaction mixture, less than half the reaction mixture, or a small amount of the reaction mixture around the photothermal nanoparticles. 7. The method of claim 3, comprising irradiating the reaction mixture with a light source at a wavelength selected based on a type of geometric shape and chemical composition of the photothermal nanoparticles. 8. The method of claim 3, comprising irradiating the reaction mixture with a light emitting diode (LED) or laser with wavelength ranges from 300nm to 1500nm. 9. The method of claim 3, comprising forming the reaction mixture with the heating media that include the photothermal nanoparticles with a particular geometry and a particular chemical coating sufficient to maintain stability under irradiation and to maintain a high photo-to-thermal conversion efficiency. 10. The method of claim 3, comprising forming the reaction mixture with the heating media comprising the photothermal nanoparticles that include metals with a core-shell of organic and/or inorganic materials. 11. The method of claim 3, comprising forming the reaction mixture with the heating media comprising the photothermal nanoparticles that include metals with an organic surface coating. 12. The method of claim 3, comprising forming the reaction mixture with the heating media comprising the photothermal nanoparticles that include alloys with an inorganic surface coating. 13. The method of claim 3, comprising forming the reaction mixture with the heating media comprising the photothermal nanoparticles that include alloys with an organic surface coating. 14. The method of claim 3, comprising forming the reaction mixture with the heating media comprising the photothermal nanoparticles that include metals selected from the group consisting of: gold (Au), silver (Ag), platinum (Pt), palladium (Pd), iron (Fe), copper (Cu), aluminum (Al), and zinc (Zn), core-shell, multi-shell, alloys, and combinations thereof. 15. The method of claim 14, comprising forming the reaction mixture with the heating media comprising the photothermal nanoparticles that include metals selected from the group consisting of: Au, Ag, Pt, Pd, Fe, Cu, Al, Zn, and combinations thereof. 16. The method of claim 3, comprising forming the reaction mixture with the heating media comprising the photothermal nanoparticles having a non-spherical geometry. 17. The method of claim 3, comprising forming the reaction mixture with the heating media comprising the photothermal nanoparticles having a non-rod shaped geometry. 18. The method of claim 3, comprising forming the reaction mixture with the heating media comprising the photothermal nanoparticles having a non-bipyramid shaped geometry. 19. The method of claim 3, comprising forming the reaction mixture with the heating media comprising the photothermal nanoparticles having a geometry selected from a group of shapes consisting of: nanostar, nanotriangle or prism, nanocube, nanoring, nanocage, hollow nanoparticles, nanoplate, polyhedron, concave nanocube, nanobar, hexagonal plates, triangular rings, hexagonal rings, and rice-like shapes. 20. The method of claim 3, comprising coating the photothermal nanoparticles with an organic material sufficient to prevent interference in the reaction. 21. The method of claim 20, wherein the organic material is selected from the group consisting of: synthetic polymers, oligo or polyethylene glycol, peptides, polystyrene and polysaccharides. 22. The method of claim 3, comprising coating the photothermal nanoparticles with an inorganic material sufficient to prevent interference in the reaction. 23. The method of claim 22, wherein the inorganic material is selected from silica and silicon. 24. The method of claim 3, comprising coating the photothermal nanoparticles with an organic material sufficient to enhance stability of the photothermal nanoparticles under irradiation and during storage. 25. The method of claim 3, comprising coating the photothermal nanoparticles with an inorganic material sufficient to enhance stability of the photothermal nanoparticles under irradiation and during storage. 26. The method of claim 3, comprising forming the reaction mixture with the heating media comprising the photothermal nanoparticles having a nanostar geometry and made essentially of gold. 27. The method of claim 3, comprising forming the reaction mixture with the heating media comprising the photothermal nanoparticles having a nanocage or hollow nanoparticle geometry and made essentially of gold or a gold-silver alloy. 28. The method of claim 3, comprising forming the reaction mixture with the heating media comprising the photothermal nanoparticles having a nanoprism/nanotriangle geometry and made essentially of silver, gold, silver coated with gold, or a gold-silver alloy. 29. The method of claim 3, comprising forming the reaction mixture with the heating media comprising the photothermal nanoparticles having a nanoring geometry and made essentially of gold or a gold-silver alloy. 30. The method of claim 3, comprising forming the reaction mixture with the heating media comprising the photothermal nanoparticles having a hexagonal ring geometry with a cavity of a different shape and diameter and made essentially of gold or a gold-silver alloy. 31. The method of claim 3, comprising forming the reaction mixture with the heating media comprising the photothermal nanoparticles having a triangular ring geometry and made essentially of gold or a gold-silver alloy. 32. The method of claim 3, comprising forming the reaction mixture with the heating media comprising the photothermal nanoparticles having a hexagonal plate-shaped geometry and made essentially of silver, gold, silver coated with gold, or a gold-silver alloy. 33. The method of claim 3, comprising forming the reaction mixture with the heating media comprising the photothermal nanoparticles having a nanohexapod geometry and made essentially of silver, gold, silver coated with gold, or a gold-silver alloy. 34. The method of claim 3, comprising forming the reaction mixture with the heating media comprising the photothermal nanoparticles having a disk geometry and made essentially of silver, gold, silver coated with gold, or a gold-silver alloy. 35. The method of claim 3, comprising forming the reaction mixture with the heating media comprising the photothermal nanoparticles fabricated on the multi-well plate, wherein the photothermal nanoparticles have a nanoring geometry and are made essentially of gold or a gold-silver alloy. 36. The method of claim 3, comprising forming the reaction mixture with the heating media comprising the photothermal nanoparticles fabricated on the multi-well plate, wherein the photothermal nanoparticles have a nanocage geometry and are made essentially of gold or a gold-silver alloy. 37. The method of claim 3, comprising forming the reaction mixture with the heating media comprising the photothermal nanoparticles fabricated on the multi-well plate, wherein the photothermal nanoparticles have a nanostar geometry and are made essentially of gold. 38. The method of claim 3, comprising forming the reaction mixture with the heating media comprising the photothermal nanoparticles fabricated on the multi-well plate, wherein the photothermal nanoparticles have a hexagonal ring geometry with a cavity of a different shape and diameter and are made essentially of gold or a gold-silver alloy. 39. The method of claim 3, comprising forming the reaction mixture with the heating media comprising the photothermal nanoparticles fabricated on the multi-well plate, wherein the photothermal nanoparticles have a triangular ring geometry and are made essentially of gold or a gold-silver alloy. 40. The method of claim 3, comprising forming the reaction mixture with the heating media comprising the photothermal nanoparticles fabricated on the multi-well plate, wherein the photothermal nanoparticles have a hexagonal plate-shaped geometry and are made essentially of silver, gold, silver coated with gold, or a gold-silver alloy. 41. The method of claim 3, comprising forming the reaction mixture with the heating media comprising the photothermal nanoparticles fabricated on the multi-well plate, wherein the photothermal nanoparticles have a nanohexapod geometry and are made essentially of silver, gold, silver coated with gold, or a gold-silver alloy. 42. The method of claim 3, comprising forming the reaction mixture with the heating media comprising the photothermal nanoparticles fabricated on the multi-well plate, wherein the photothermal nanoparticles have a nanorod geometry and are made essentially of gold. 43. The method of claim 3, comprising forming the reaction mixture with the heating media comprising the photothermal nanoparticles fabricated on the multi-well plate, wherein the photothermal nanoparticles have a nanospherical geometry and are made essentially of gold. 44. A method for rapid amplification of a nucleic acid molecule with a polymerase chain reaction by: forming a reaction mixture including a nucleic acid template, a polymerase enzyme, and a heating media including photothermal nanoparticles on a microchip or surface of a plate, 

 wherein the photothermal nanoparticles have a particular geometric shape and comprises a single component or a multi-component, and the photothermal nanoparticles are further categorized by surface plasmon resonance with a resonance wavelength in a particular range to convert energy absorbed from a light source to sufficiently heat a volume of a composition for transcription of a new nucleic acid strand, and wherein the particular geometric shape is selected from the group consisting of: a polyhedron, a spheroid, and a torus; and hollow shapes. irradiating the reaction mixture at a wavelengths ranging from 300 nm to 1500 nm depending on the particular geometric shape of the photothermal nanoparticles, sufficient for heating the reaction mixture at a rate up to 75 degrees Celsius per second to complete amplification thermal cycles in 15 minutes or less time by adjusting a concentration of photothermal nanoparticles. 45. A photothermal nanoparticle having a particular geometric shape and comprising a single component nanoparticle or a multi-component nanoparticle, wherein the photothermal nanoparticle is further categorized by surface plasmon resonance with a resonance wavelength in a particular range to convert energy absorbed from a light source to heat, and wherein the particular geometric shape is selected from the group consisting of: polyhedral, spheroid, toms, and hollow shapes. 46. The photothermal nanoparticle of claim 45, wherein the particular geometric shape includes a polyhedron having at least one curved face. 47. The photothermal nanoparticle of claim 45, wherein the resonance wavelength is in the range of 300 nm to 1500 nm. 48. The photothermal nanoparticle of claim 45, wherein the photothermal nanoparticle is a single component polyhedral nanoparticle. 49. The photothermal nanoparticle of claim 45, wherein the photothermal nanoparticle is a multi-component polyhedral nanoparticle. 50. The photothermal nanoparticle of claim 45, wherein the photothermal nanoparticle is a multi-component nanoparticle including an alloy. 51. The photothermal nanoparticle of claim 45, wherein the photothermal nanoparticle is a multi-component polyhedral nanoparticle including a plasmonic core and more than one shell layer. 52. The photothermal nanoparticle of claim 45, wherein the photothermal nanoparticle is a multi-component polyhedral nanoparticle including a plasmonic core and at least one shell layer. 53. The photothermal nanoparticle of claim 45, wherein the photothermal nanoparticle is a spheroid nanoparticle characterized by a rice-like shape. 54. The photothermal nanoparticle of claim 45, wherein the photothermal nanoparticle is a polyhedral nanoparticle characterized by a nanostar shape. 55. The photothermal nanoparticle of claim 45, wherein the photothermal nanoparticle is a polyhedral nanoparticle characterized by a nanocube shape. 56. The photothermal nanoparticle of claim 45, wherein the photothermal nanoparticle is a polyhedral nanoparticle characterized by a nanoprism shape. 57. The photothermal nanoparticle of claim 45, wherein the photothermal nanoparticle is a polyhedral nanoparticle characterized by a polyhedron frame with a hollow core. 58. The photothermal nanoparticle of claim 45, wherein the photothermal nanoparticle is a polyhedral nanoparticle having a plurality of faces, wherein at least two of the plurality of faces have a different shape or size as compared to a remainder of the plurality of faces. 59. The photothermal nanoparticle of claim 45, wherein the photothermal nanoparticle is a polyhedral nanoparticle having a plurality of faces, wherein at least three of the plurality of faces have a different shape or size as compared to a remainder of the plurality of faces. 60. The photothermal nanoparticle of claim 45, wherein the photothermal nanoparticle is a polyhedral nanoparticle having at least two non-symmetrical portions. 61. The photothermal nanoparticle of claim 45, further characterized by photothermal properties sufficient for increasing a temperature of a mixture in contact with the photothermal nanoparticle from 55 degrees Celsius (C) to 95 degrees C within 1 second. 62. The photothermal nanoparticle of claim 45, further characterized by photothermal properties sufficient for increasing a temperature of a mixture in contact with the photothermal nanoparticle from 55 degrees Celsius (C) to 95 degrees C within 2 seconds. 63. The photothermal nanoparticle of claim 45, further characterized by photothermal properties sufficient for increasing a temperature of a mixture in contact with the photothermal nanoparticle from 55 degrees Celsius (C) to 95 degrees C within 4 seconds. 64. The photothermal nanoparticle of claim 45, further having photothermal properties sufficient for increasing a temperature of a mixture in contact with the photothermal nanoparticle at a rate of up to 75 degrees Celsius (C) per second. 65. The photothermal nanoparticle of claim 45, further having photothermal properties sufficient for increasing a temperature of a mixture in contact with the photothermal nanoparticle at a rate of up to 40 degrees Celsius (C) per second. 66. The photothermal nanoparticle of claim 45, further having photothermal properties sufficient for increasing a temperature of a mixture in contact with the photothermal nanoparticle at a rate of up to 20 degrees Celsius (C) per second. 67 The photothermal nanoparticle of claim 45, further having photothermal properties sufficient for increasing a temperature of a mixture in contact with the photothermal nanoparticle at a rate of up to 10 degrees Celsius (C) per second. 68. A composition, comprising: at least one first type of plasmonic photothermal nanoparticle having a particular geometric shape and comprising a single component or a multi-component, wherein the first type of plasmonic photothermal nanoparticle is further categorized by surface plasmon resonance with a resonance wavelength in a particular range to convert energy absorbed from 

 a light source to heat, and wherein the particular geometric shape is selected from the group consisting of: polyhedral, spheroid, torus, and hollow shapes at least one second type of plasmonic photothermal nanoparticle having a different particular geometric shape as compared to the first type of plasmonic photothermal nanoparticle. 69. The composition of claim 68, wherein the second type of plasmonic photothermal nanoparticle consists of a single component or a multi-component nanoparticle, wherein the second type of plasmonic photothermal nanoparticle is further categorized by surface plasmon resonance with a resonance wavelength in a particular range to convert energy absorbed from a light source to heat, and wherein the particular geometric shape is selected from the group consisting of: polyhedral, spheroid, torus, and hollow shapes. 70. The composition of claim 68, further comprising a relative ratio of the first type of plasmonic photothermal nanoparticle and the second type of plasmonic photothermal nanoparticle to target a particular peak resonance wavelength. 71. The composition of claim 68, further comprising a relative ratio of the first type of plasmonic photothermal nanoparticle and the second type of plasmonic photothermal nanoparticle to target a peak resonance wavelength sufficient for at least one polymerase chain reaction process. 72. A composition, comprising: a reaction reagent including deoxyribonucleotide triphosphates (dNTPs) and a buffer solution; a polymerase enzyme configured to transcribe a new nucleic acid strand from the dNTPs; and a plurality of plasmonic photothermal nanoparticles having a particular geometric shape and comprising a single component or a multi-component wherein the plurality of plasmonic photothermal nanoparticles are further categorized by surface plasmon resonance with a resonance wavelength in a particular range to convert energy absorbed from a light source to sufficiently heat a volume of the composition for transcription of the new nucleic acid strand, and wherein the particular geometric shape is selected from the group consisting of: polyhedral, spheroid, toms, and hollow shapes. 73. The composition of claim 72, wherein the reaction reagent further includes magnesium. 74. The composition of claim 72, wherein the reaction reagent further includes magnesium chloride. 75. The composition of claim 72, wherein the reaction reagent further includes a stabilizing agent. 76. A kit for transcription of a nucleic acid strand, comprising: a first volume including a reaction reagent including nucleoside triphosphates (NTPs) and a buffer solution; a second volume including a polymerase enzyme configured to transcribe a new nucleic acid strand from the NTPs; and a third volume including a plurality of plasmonic photothermal nanoparticles having a particular geometric shape and comprising a single component or a multi-component, wherein the plurality of plasmonic photothermal nanoparticles are further categorized by surface plasmon resonance with a resonance wavelength in a particular range to convert energy absorbed from a light source to sufficiently heat a volume of the first volume, the second volume, and the third volume for the transcription of the new nucleic acid strand, and wherein the particular geometric shape is selected from the group consisting of: polyhedral, spheroid, toms, and hollow shapes. 77. A kit for transcription of a nucleic acid strand, comprising: a first volume including a reaction reagent including nucleoside triphosphates (NTPs) and a buffer solution; a second volume including: a polymerase enzyme configured to transcribe a new nucleic acid strand from the NTPs; and a plurality of plasmonic photothermal nanoparticles having a particular geometric shape and comprising a single component or a multi-component, wherein the plurality of plasmonic photothermal nanoparticles are further categorized by surface plasmon resonance with a resonance wavelength in a particular range to convert energy absorbed from a light 

 source to sufficiently heat a volume of the first volume, and the second volume for the transcription of the new nucleic acid strand, and wherein the particular geometric shape is selected from the group consisting of: polyhedral, spheroid, torus, and hollow shapes.
